title: Never conflate loading, not-found, and empty states
tip: When building data-dependent UI, always distinguish Loading (data fetch in progress), Not Found (fetch completed, resource does not exist or user lacks access), and Empty (fetch completed, resource exists but has no items). Conflating these causes Trip Not Found regressions during auth hydration.
avoid_when: static pages with no data dependencies
signals: flash of error state on page load
Trip Not Found shown briefly then resolves
empty state shown while data is loading
evidence: Documented as zero-tolerance path in CLAUDE.md. Recurring regression pattern in Chravel.

title: Trace field names end-to-end before patching data bugs
tip: When a data value renders incorrectly, trace the field from DB schema to Supabase types to hook/query to component props to render. Fix at the source layer, not via mapping hacks. Field name mismatches between layers are a historically common Chravel regression.
avoid_when: pure UI/styling bugs with no data dependency
evidence: Documented in AGENTS.md section 5.2 as a stop-the-line bug class.

title: Trip Not Found flash during auth hydration
tip: When users see a brief Trip Not Found flash on valid trip URLs, the root cause is almost always the trip data fetch firing before auth session hydration completes. Gate data fetches on auth being fully resolved, not just present.
subtasks: log auth state at query time
signals: brief error flash then correct content
query returns null before auth resolves
Trip Not Found component mounts then unmounts
evidence: Zero-tolerance path in CLAUDE.md. Historical recurring regression.

title: Treat imports and sync as state machines, not parser calls
tip: For external data ingestion, define explicit run states (received/parsed/deduped/staged/confirmed/materialized) with idempotency keys and partial terminal states. UI toasts alone are insufficient because retries, replayed events, and parser ambiguity can create silent corruption.
evidence: March 2026 integrations/import-export audit found fragmented semantics across gmail-import-worker, file-ai-parser, and export pipelines.

title: Gate all data fetches on fully hydrated auth session
tip: Supabase Auth returns null during session hydration, which is indistinguishable from logged out. All data fetches on auth-gated routes must wait for session hydration to complete (not just check session !== null). The useAuth hook tracks hydration state; use its isLoading/isReady signal before enabling queries.
applies_when: any TanStack Query with auth dependency
subtasks: verify query enabled flag gates on auth resolved
test with hard refresh on auth-gated route
confirm no flash of error during hydration
signals: data fetch returns 401 on page load then succeeds
Trip Not Found flash on valid URLs
evidence: Zero-tolerance path in CLAUDE.md. useAuth.tsx is the single auth provider (46KB). Historical recurring regression documented in DEBUG_PATTERNS.md.

title: Backfill messages on Supabase Realtime reconnect
tip: Supabase Realtime does NOT replay missed events after a WebSocket disconnect. When the channel status returns to SUBSCRIBED or on visibilitychange (tab refocus), fetch messages since the last known server timestamp and merge with deduplication by id or client_message_id. Without this, users lose messages received during connectivity gaps.
applies_when: any realtime subscription where data loss is unacceptable
avoid_when: read-only displays that refresh on mount
ephemeral UI state like typing indicators
subtasks: track last known server timestamp per channel
listen for SUBSCRIBED status after reconnect
fetch gap messages and merge with dedup
evidence: LESSONS.md entry on realtime reconnect backfill. DEBUG_PATTERNS.md chat reliability section. Supabase Realtime documented limitation.

title: Enforce payment SDK boundary: RevenueCat for iOS, Stripe for web
tip: RevenueCat handles iOS/Android subscriptions; Stripe handles web checkout and webhooks. Never mix client SDKs across platforms. The subscription source of truth is the user_subscriptions table populated by webhooks, not client-side SDK state. Gate premium features on DB subscription status queried via hooks, not on RevenueCat/Stripe client cache which can drift.
subtasks: verify SDK platform detection in revenuecatClient.ts
check entitlement queries go through DB not SDK cache
test paywall renders correctly per platform
signals: subscription shows as active on one platform but not another
entitlement check passes client-side but fails server-side
evidence: RevenueCat integration in src/integrations/revenuecat/revenuecatClient.ts with platform detection. Stripe edge functions for web checkout. LESSONS.md SDK boot compatibility entry.

title: AI concierge writes go through pending actions buffer
tip: AI-initiated writes to shared trip state (tasks, polls, calendar events, places) must go through the trip_pending_actions buffer, not direct inserts. The user confirms or rejects each pending action. Use tool_call_id as an idempotency key to prevent duplicate actions from retries. Read-only operations (search, recommendations, lookups) bypass the buffer.
applies_when: any AI-initiated mutation to shared trip data
avoid_when: AI responses that only display information
subtasks: verify tool writes target pending_actions table
test retry does not create duplicate action
signals: AI writes appear without user confirmation
tool call creates data in wrong table
evidence: AUDIT_CONCIERGE_LIVE.md documents tool call safety. AI concierge tools and guardrails doc in docs/AI_CONCIERGE_TOOLS_AND_GUARDRAILS.md.

title: Permission model varies by trip type: consumer open, pro role-based, event organizer-only
tip: Consumer trips allow all members to mutate. Pro trips use role-based permissions (admin/member/viewer). Event trips are organizer-only for mutations. Use useMutationPermissions(tripId) which resolves trip type once and returns flat boolean flags. This is client-side UX gating only; RLS policies are the authoritative enforcement layer. Never trust client-side permission checks for security.
applies_when: any mutation hook serving multiple trip types
avoid_when: features that only apply to one trip type
subtasks: verify RLS policy matches client permission logic
test mutation access across all three trip types
confirm unauthorized mutations are blocked server-side
signals: mutation succeeds in UI but fails at DB level
wrong permission level for trip type
evidence: docs/ACTIVE/SHARED_MUTATION_AUDIT.md documents the unified permission hook applied to 5 mutation hooks. LESSONS.md unified permission guard entry.

title: Calendar sync uses sync tokens and etags for idempotent bi-directional sync
tip: Google Calendar sync is bi-directional. Use sync_token for incremental sync and etag for conflict detection. Never overwrite remote events without comparing timestamps. The calendar-sync edge function is the authority for sync operations; client-side calendarService.ts reads/writes through Supabase, not directly to Google. Expired sync tokens require a full re-sync.
avoid_when: local-only calendar views with no sync
subtasks: verify sync token usage in edge function
signals: event edits not propagating to Google
evidence: supabase/functions/calendar-sync/ edge function. docs/CALENDAR_ARCHITECTURE_REVIEW.md. calendarService.ts handles client-side queries.

title: Single Google Maps instance per page with debounced events
tip: Only one Google Maps instance per page. Use MapView component with mode props for different contexts (trip map, place search, location sharing). Always null-check mapRef.current before operations. Debounce high-frequency events (drag, zoom, bounds_changed) at 300ms minimum. Clean up all event listeners in useEffect return.
signals: multiple map instances on same page
janky map interaction from undebounced events
evidence: CLAUDE.md Google Maps Rules section. docs/SINGLE_MAP_ARCHITECTURE.md architecture doc.

title: Notification dual-path dedup prevents duplicate delivery
tip: Notifications can be generated by both database triggers and edge functions. Without deduplication, broadcasts and other multi-recipient events can fire duplicate push/email/in-app notifications. The notification system uses centralized dispatch with preference gating, quiet hours, and rate limiting. When adding new notification-generating features, use exactly one generation path per event type.
applies_when: any feature that sends notifications to multiple users
avoid_when: features that consume but don't generate notifications
subtasks: verify single generation path per event
verify rate limiting on high-fanout events
evidence: NOTIFICATION_AUDIT.md documents dual-path dedup fix for broadcasts. Pattern applies to all new notification-generating features.

title: Gemini Live voice sessions require explicit lifecycle cleanup
tip: Gemini Live voice sessions use WebSocket connections with audio streaming state. Sessions must be explicitly closed on component unmount, navigation, and error. Audio state (microphone, playback) can get stuck if cleanup is skipped. The useGeminiLive hook manages session lifecycle; always use its cleanup functions rather than managing WebSocket directly. Voice sessions are separate from text concierge and dictation features.
applies_when: component unmount with active voice session
subtasks: check audio state reset on session end
verify error handling closes session cleanly
signals: microphone stays active after leaving page
audio playback continues after session end
evidence: AUDIT_CONCIERGE_LIVE.md found dead code in VoiceLiveOverlay and reconnect state issues. useGeminiLive.ts (54KB) manages full session lifecycle.

title: Media uploads use compression pipeline before storage
tip: Media uploads go through a client-side compression pipeline before being sent to Supabase Storage. Large images are resized and compressed; videos may have size limits. The mediaService handles upload orchestration while the image-upload edge function handles server-side processing. AI tagging via Gemini is async and failures are silently tolerated (non-blocking). Storage bucket policies enforce access control via RLS.
subtasks: verify AI tagging failure is non-blocking
evidence: src/services/mediaService.ts handles upload orchestration. supabase/functions/image-upload/ for server processing. Media AI tagging in mediaAITagging.ts.
